And just as a point of reference for those of you who don't know, this is old-fashioned, and forget legitimacy, illegitimacy, but in three and a half years these guys put 500 and some odd thousand people into a business at $5,000 a piece. And of course, no internet, no webinars, no Google, hell, no tele-seminars. You couldn't even do tele-seminars, though. Not the most people I think you'd have on a conference call was like 50 people. So these guys did it all with old-fashioned manual labor, putting people in rooms doing meetings. And a half a million people is a ton of people at $5,000 a clip. I don't care if you're selling them how to breed chicken feathers at home and make money. It doesn't make any difference. It's a ton of people. And they pretty much did it on the strength of this guy and his persona and his story. And he didn't invent this stuff either, by the way. There's a long lineage of this. It goes back to William Penn Patrick and before him. But there's the shack that we grew up in. There's the castle. Tony, not the first guy to think of the castle thing. There's the big crowds. Big crowds are very good proof of importance. Very good. There's film of... You know how a big crowd will meet like a presidential candidate at an airport and they'll have a rally at an airport and when a plane lands and he comes off the plane, there's all these people gathered around to see the political candidate. They replicated that in every city for him. Proof of importance. Book signings can be proof of importance. Media appearances can be proof of importance. So those are the three elements. One to eight. A difficult but intriguing proposition made simple for the prospect. So sometimes we are selling difficult to understand propositions. In the pure opportunity world, so she mentioned Graziano when she was up here. So get rich in real estate is the oldest. The most enduring of the pure opportunities. Why is that? Because everybody gets it. Everybody lives in a house or an apartment. Everybody's going to buy from you. Well, unless you're right Thurston, then homeless people are coming to the seminars and buying but that's inside baseball that none of you will get. But there is a get rich in real estate guy. I spoke at five seminars for him who strategically locates the seminar locations by bus stops so the prospects can get there. I'm not kidding. But for the most part, everybody lives in a house. They get it. They know somebody who lives in a house. And almost everybody has somebody in their family who owns a rental property. So get rich in real estate works real, real well, always has worked real well because everybody gets it. It's not complicated for people to understand what the hell it is that we're talking about. Everybody knows houses get bought. Everybody knows houses get sold. Everybody knows houses get rented. There's a base of knowledge. Get rich in mail order used to be almost like that because there really was mail order. So there was this basic frame of reference of the Sears and Roebuck catalog. And so when you said mail order, it was almost like say in real estate. So for a number of decades, get rich in mail order was like second behind get rich in real estate. It all got very complicated because there is really no mail order. There's direct marketing and there's internet and there's even. And so today if you say mail order, unless you're selling to 65 and up, you're either viewed as being antiquated or really it doesn't have meaning. And so it's harder to explain. Info marketing now has always been around. Some people call it self publishing for a number of years, but it's always been there in the opportunity category, but it's always been tougher to put across because people don't have a ready reference for it. It has to be explained. So it's an example of something in the opportunity world that is a little more difficult. So how do we get the difficult proposition across if we're stuck with the difficult proposition to get across? One which we're coming to is analogy, but another that is brilliantly used often. I made reference to it earlier. So if you flip to page to 182 and it got copied twice, got help us. I don't know why. Let me see if I do. Oh, so you could see the whole ad. Okay, so 182 and 183. So this really is an ad and it's old. This is an ad for info marketing to sell the opportunity of information marketing, which is now a more difficult proposition to explain than get rich in real estate. So one of the clever techniques used by people for those kinds of opportunities that does have transference for all info marketing is right in the middle of page, the profit test. This is really a demonstration. So it's worth taking a second here and reading it before we go on. So this demonstrates and makes clear what it is that this opportunity is about. When you're done, if you flip to 185, you'll see another classic opportunity ad. There have always been four or five major players in this category and their opportunity is how to be a writer, which is even harder to put across A, because it's harder to understand and B, the whole personal possibility, personal attainability, that gets more and more and more difficult. There's a lot of people who would like to do it, but a lot of people simply don't think they can.
